---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
#CondenScreen Low-throughput validation analysis script
#Date: 2/11/2026
#Version: 2024.12.1+563
#Author: Dylan Poch
############################################################
### ================= USER SETTINGS ===================== ###
############################################################
 
base_path  <- "C:/Your/Path/Here/"
#base_path <- "C:/Users/Schlieker Lab/Desktop/New_Data_AFTER_23_11_17/Dylan_new/Random_projects/26_-1_31_CBB_dp_EXPORT/Results/" #Example
input_file <- "YourCellProfilerExport.csv"
#input_file <- "CBBNuclei.csv"   #Example
negative_control_label <- "DMSO"   # EXACT name in Condition column

plot_title_violin <- "MLF2-GFP Foci"
plot_title_bar    <- "MLF2-GFP Foci Distribution"
plot_title_cns    <- "CNS Score Distribution:"

output_prefix <- "Phenotype_Analysis"

top_fraction <- 0.50   # optional filtering if activated

low_cutoff <- 30
mid_cutoff <- 75

y_axis_label_violin <- "MLF2-GFP Count per Cell"
y_axis_label_bar    <- "Proportion of Cells"

############################################################
### ================= LOAD PACKAGES ===================== ###
############################################################

required_pkgs <- c("openxlsx","ggplot2","dplyr","tidyr","ggpubr","rstatix","scales")

for (pkg in required_pkgs) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}

windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))  # ensures Arial works on Windows


############################################################
### ================= LOAD & PREP DATA ================= ###
############################################################

df_raw <- read.csv(file.path(base_path, input_file))

 
  
df <- df_raw %>%
  select(
    Image  = ImageNumber,
    Cell   = ObjectNumber,
    File   = FileName_DAPI,
    any_of(c("Children_Foci_Count", "Children_GFP_foci_Count")),
    any_of("Intensity_IntegratedIntensity_tdtomato")
  )

if ("Children_Foci_Count" %in% colnames(df)) {
  df <- df %>% rename(Foci = Children_Foci_Count)
} else if ("Children_GFP_foci_Count" %in% colnames(df)) {
  df <- df %>% rename(Foci = Children_GFP_foci_Count)
}

if ("Intensity_IntegratedIntensity_tdtomato" %in% colnames(df)) {
  df <- df %>%
    rename(TdTomato_Intensity = Intensity_IntegratedIntensity_tdtomato)
}

# Assign experimental condition based on filename pattern
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Condition = case_when(
    grepl("Nif_100uM", File) ~ "Condition A",
    grepl("DMSO", File) ~ "DMSO",
   #grepl("Condition_C", File) ~ "Condition C"
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Condition))   # removes everything not explicitly listed

sub_df2 <- df   # master dataset


############################################################
### ===== OPTIONAL: KEEP TOP FRACTION PER CONDITION ===== ###
### (Uncomment to activate. Overwrites sub_df2)          ###
############################################################

# sub_df2 <- sub_df2 %>%
#   group_by(Condition) %>%
#   arrange(Foci, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
#   mutate(rank = row_number(),
#          cutoff = ceiling(n() * top_fraction)) %>%
#   filter(rank > cutoff) %>%
#   ungroup()


############################################################
### ================= VIOLIN PLOT ====================== ###
############################################################

# Summary stats for reporting
summary_stats <- sub_df2 %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(
    N_Cells = n(),
    Mean_Foci = mean(Foci),
    Median_Foci = median(Foci),
    SD_Foci = sd(Foci),
    SEM_Foci = SD_Foci / sqrt(N_Cells),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Mann–Whitney U tests vs leftmost condition
sub_df2$Condition <- factor(sub_df2$Condition, levels = unique(sub_df2$Condition))

stat_tests <- sub_df2 %>%
  wilcox_test(Foci ~ Condition, ref.group = levels(sub_df2$Condition)[1]) %>%
  adjust_pvalue(method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance("p.adj")

# Stagger p-value brackets
y_max <- max(sub_df2$Foci, na.rm = TRUE)
stat_tests <- stat_tests %>%
  arrange(group2) %>%
  mutate(y.position = y_max * (1.05 + row_number() * 0.06))

# Locked color palette
conditions <- levels(sub_df2$Condition)
n_cond <- length(conditions)

gray   <- "#4A4A4A"
blue   <- "#6E97AD"
purple <- "#6B5A8E"

if (n_cond == 1) fill_colors <- gray else
if (n_cond == 2) fill_colors <- c(gray, blue) else
if (n_cond == 3) fill_colors <- c(gray, blue, purple) else {
  grad_cols <- colorRampPalette(c(gray, blue, purple))(n_cond)
  grad_cols[1] <- gray
  grad_cols[round(n_cond/2)] <- blue
  grad_cols[n_cond] <- purple
  fill_colors <- grad_cols
}
names(fill_colors) <- conditions

# Compute medians for solid lines
median_df <- sub_df2 %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(med = median(Foci), .groups = "drop")

# Build violin plot
plot_violin <- ggviolin(sub_df2, x="Condition", y="Foci",
                        fill="Condition", trim=TRUE, alpha=0.85, color="black") +
  geom_jitter(width=0.12, size=1.5, alpha=0.6) +
  geom_segment(data=median_df,
               aes(x=as.numeric(Condition)-0.25, xend=as.numeric(Condition)+0.25,
                   y=med, yend=med), inherit.aes=FALSE,
               color="black", linewidth=0.7) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(stat_tests, label="p.adj.signif",
                     bracket.size=0.4, tip.length=0.01, size=7, color="black") +
  labs(title=plot_title_violin, y=y_axis_label_violin, x=NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=fill_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size=16) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial", face="bold"),
        plot.title=element_text(size=22, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.y=element_text(size=20),
        axis.text.y=element_text(size=18),
        axis.text.x=element_text(angle=25, hjust=1),
        legend.position="right")



ggsave(file.path(base_path, paste0(output_prefix, "_ViolinPlot.png")),
       plot_violin, width=10, height=8, dpi=300)


############################################################
### ================= STACKED BAR PLOT ================= ###
############################################################
# Bin foci counts
binned_df <- sub_df2 %>%
  mutate(Foci_bin = case_when(
    Foci <= low_cutoff ~ paste0("0–", low_cutoff, " foci"),
    Foci > low_cutoff & Foci < mid_cutoff ~ paste0(low_cutoff, "–", mid_cutoff, " foci"),
    Foci >= mid_cutoff ~ paste0(">", mid_cutoff, " foci")
  ))

# Proportion per bin
totals <- binned_df %>%
  count(Condition, name="total_cells")

bin_summary <- binned_df %>%
  count(Condition, Foci_bin, name="cells_in_bin") %>%
  left_join(totals, by="Condition") %>%
  mutate(prop = cells_in_bin / total_cells)

# Highest foci at bottom of stack
bin_levels <- c(
 paste0("0–", low_cutoff, " foci"),
  paste0(low_cutoff, "–", mid_cutoff, " foci"),
   paste0(">", mid_cutoff, " foci")
)

# Match color order to bin_levels - darker blue for highest bin (first/bottom)
bin_colors <- c( "grey75", "grey40", "#2C6A88")
names(bin_colors) <- bin_levels

# Convert Foci_bin to factor with specified levels
bin_summary <- bin_summary %>%
  mutate(Foci_bin = factor(Foci_bin, levels = bin_levels))

plot_bar <- ggplot(bin_summary, aes(x=Condition, y=prop, fill=Foci_bin)) +
  geom_col(width=0.7, color="black", alpha=0.95) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format(accuracy=1), 
                     expand=expansion(mult=c(0,0.05))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=bin_colors, name="GFP foci per cell") +
  labs(title=plot_title_bar, y=y_axis_label_bar, x=NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size=16) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial", face="bold"),
        plot.title=element_text(size=22, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.y=element_text(size=20),
        axis.text.y=element_text(size=18),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size=16, angle=25, hjust=1),
        legend.position="right")

ggsave(file.path(base_path, paste0(output_prefix, "_StackedBars.png")),
       plot_bar, width=10, height=8, dpi=300)




############################################################
### ===== OPTIONAL: ADDITIONAL VIOLIN PLOTS (CNS + TDT) ###
### (Safe to run; only executes if data columns exist)   ###
############################################################

# Ensure Condition factor order matches main plot
sub_df2$Condition <- factor(sub_df2$Condition, levels = unique(sub_df2$Condition))

# Reuse same color palette from main violin
extra_fill_colors <- fill_colors



############################################################
### ================= EXPORT TO EXCEL ================== ###
############################################################

wb <- createWorkbook()

addWorksheet(wb, "Filtered_Data")
writeData(wb, "Filtered_Data", sub_df2)

addWorksheet(wb, "Summary_Stats")
writeData(wb, "Summary_Stats", summary_stats)

addWorksheet(wb, "Statistical_Tests")
writeData(wb, "Statistical_Tests", stat_tests)

addWorksheet(wb, "StackedBar_Proportions")
writeData(wb, "StackedBar_Proportions", bin_summary)





##############################
# ---  TdTomato Intensity ---
##############################

if ("TdTomato_Intensity" %in% colnames(sub_df2)) {

 
  # Stats vs control
  stat_tdt <- sub_df2 %>%
    wilcox_test(TdTomato_Intensity ~ Condition,
                ref.group = levels(sub_df2$Condition)[1]) %>%
    adjust_pvalue(method = "BH") %>%
    add_significance("p.adj")

  y_max_tdt <- max(sub_df2$TdTomato_Intensity, na.rm = TRUE)

  stat_tdt <- stat_tdt %>%
    arrange(group2) %>%
    mutate(y.position = y_max_tdt * (1.05 + row_number() * 0.06))

  median_tdt <- sub_df2 %>%
    group_by(Condition) %>%
    summarise(med = median(TdTomato_Intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  plot_tdt <- ggviolin(sub_df2, x="Condition", y="TdTomato_Intensity",
                       fill="Condition", trim=TRUE, alpha=0.85, color="black") +
    geom_jitter(width=0.12, size=1.5, alpha=0.6) +
    geom_segment(data=median_tdt,
                 aes(x=as.numeric(Condition)-0.25, xend=as.numeric(Condition)+0.25,
                     y=med, yend=med),
                 inherit.aes=FALSE, color="black", linewidth=0.7) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(stat_tdt, label="p.adj.signif",
                       bracket.size=0.4, tip.length=0.01, size=7, color="black") +
    labs(title="TdTomato Integrated Intensity",
         y="Integrated Intensity (TdTomato)", x=NULL) +
    scale_fill_manual(values=extra_fill_colors) +
    theme_classic(base_size=16) +
    theme(text=element_text(family="Arial", face="bold"),
          plot.title=element_text(size=22, hjust=0.5),
          axis.title.y=element_text(size=20),
          axis.text.y=element_text(size=18),
          axis.text.x=element_text(angle=25, hjust=1),
          legend.position="right") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim=c(0, max(stat_tdt$y.position)*1.1), clip="off")

  ggsave(file.path(base_path, paste0(output_prefix, "_TdTomato_Violin.png")),
         plot_tdt, width=10, height=8, dpi=300, device=ragg::agg_png)
  
    
    
  addWorksheet(wb, "TdTomato_Stats")
  writeData(wb, "TdTomato_Stats", stat_tdt)
  
  addWorksheet(wb, "TdTomato_Median")
  writeData(wb, "TdTomato_Median", median_tdt)



}


 
############################################################
### ================= CNS CALCULATION ================== ###
############################################################

if (all(c("Foci", "TdTomato_Intensity") %in% colnames(sub_df2))) {
  
  
  

  control_mean_foci <- mean(sub_df2$Foci[sub_df2$Condition == negative_control_label], na.rm = TRUE)
  control_mean_tdt  <- mean(sub_df2$TdTomato_Intensity[sub_df2$Condition == negative_control_label], na.rm = TRUE)

  sub_df2$CNS <- (1 - (sub_df2$Foci / control_mean_foci)) *
                 (sub_df2$TdTomato_Intensity / control_mean_tdt)^2

  control_median <- median(sub_df2$CNS[sub_df2$Condition == negative_control_label], na.rm = TRUE)

  total_cells <- sub_df2 %>%
    group_by(Condition) %>%
    summarise(total_n = n(), .groups = "drop")

  sub_df2_filt <- sub_df2 %>%
    filter(CNS >= control_median) %>%
    mutate(
      CNS_bin = case_when(
        CNS >= control_median & CNS < 0.25 ~ "Baseline–0.25",
        CNS >= 0.25 & CNS < 1              ~ "0.25–1",
        CNS >= 1                           ~ "1+"
      ),
      CNS_bin = factor(CNS_bin, levels = c("Baseline–0.25","0.25–1","1+"))
    )

  cns_dist <- sub_df2_filt %>%
    group_by(Condition, CNS_bin) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(total_cells, by = "Condition") %>%
    mutate(prop_total = n / total_n)

  cns_dist_plot <- cns_dist %>%
    filter(Condition != negative_control_label)
  
  
    plot_cns_binned <- ggplot(cns_dist_plot,
                            aes(x = Condition, y = prop_total, fill = CNS_bin)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.75, color = "black") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Baseline–0.25" = "#BDBDBD",
      "0.25–1"        = "#4B4B4B",
      "1+"            = "#3B4CC0"
    )) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                       limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(title = plot_title_cns,
         y = "Fraction of Total Cells",
         x = NULL,
         fill = "Rescue Strength") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "Arial", face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 22, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  ggsave(file.path(base_path, paste0(output_prefix, "_CNS_Distribution.png")),
         plot_cns_binned, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, device = ragg::agg_png)
  
  
    addWorksheet(wb, "CNS_Distribution")
  writeData(wb, "CNS_Distribution", cns_dist)

}
 

saveWorkbook(wb,
  file.path(base_path, paste0(output_prefix, "_Analysis.xlsx")),
  overwrite=TRUE)

```

 
When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
